title: Implement Centralized Identity Provider and Unified Authentication
description: Deploy and configure a centralized identity provider (e.g., Keycloak, Zitadel, or Auth0) to serve as the single source of truth for user authentication, token issuance (JWT/OIDC), and session management. Refactor all services to validate tokens against this provider and remove independent JWT verification logic. Ensure support for user registration, login, token revocation, and session termination. Update service configurations to use the new provider for authentication and authorization checks.
subtasks: Select and deploy an identity provider (Keycloak, Zitadel, or Auth0) in the cluster.
Configure user registration, login, and role management in the identity provider.
Implement OIDC/JWT token issuance and revocation endpoints.
Update all backend services to validate JWTs against the central provider's public keys.
Remove per-service JWT verification logic and secrets.
Implement token revocation and session management endpoints.
Document the authentication flow and update service documentation.
Run end-to-end authentication tests across all services.
Run OWASP ZAP scan against all user-facing endpoints to verify authentication controls.
acceptance_criteria: All services authenticate users via the centralized identity provider.
JWTs are issued and validated using the provider's public keys.
Token revocation and session termination are supported and effective across all services.
No service implements its own JWT verification logic.
End-to-end authentication and authorization tests pass for all user and admin flows.
OWASP ZAP scan shows no authentication bypass or token validation vulnerabilities.

title: Enforce PCI DSS Controls and Secure Stripe Webhook Handling
description: Harden Stripe payment processing and webhook handling to meet PCI DSS requirements. Ensure mandatory signature verification, restrict webhook endpoint access to Stripe IP ranges, implement constant-time signature comparison, and add rate limiting. Exclude raw card/payment data from logs and document PCI DSS scope and controls.
subtasks: Enforce Stripe webhook signature verification in the Finance service (fail requests without valid signature).
Implement constant-time signature comparison for webhook verification.
Configure NetworkPolicy or WAF to restrict webhook endpoint access to Stripe's published IP ranges.
Add rate limiting to the Stripe webhook endpoint.
Update audit logging configuration to exclude raw card/payment data.
Document PCI DSS scope, controls, and responsibilities in the security documentation.
Add monitoring and alerting for unusual webhook volume.
Test webhook endpoint with valid and invalid signatures and from allowed/blocked IPs.
Run OWASP ZAP scan against the webhook endpoint.
acceptance_criteria: Stripe webhook endpoint only accepts requests with valid signatures and from Stripe IP ranges.
Constant-time signature comparison is implemented and verified.
Rate limiting is enforced on the webhook endpoint.
No raw card/payment data is present in logs.
PCI DSS scope and controls are documented and reviewed.
Monitoring and alerting are active for webhook anomalies.
OWASP ZAP scan shows no vulnerabilities on the webhook endpoint.

title: Implement Encryption at Rest, Field-Level Encryption, and Data Retention for PII
description: Enable encryption at rest for all PostgreSQL data using CloudNative-PG or underlying storage encryption. Implement field-level encryption for highly sensitive PII (e.g., credit scores, personal identifiers, payroll data) at the application layer. Define and enforce data retention and deletion policies for customer and vetting data. Add a GDPR/data protection compliance review.
subtasks: Enable storage-level encryption for PostgreSQL volumes via CloudNative-PG or cloud provider.
Implement field-level encryption in application code for sensitive PII fields before database storage.
Update database schemas and application logic to support encrypted fields.
Define data retention and deletion policies for PII and financial data.
Implement automated cleanup jobs for expired data.
Conduct a GDPR/data protection compliance review and document findings.
Test encryption at rest and field-level encryption with sample data.
Verify data deletion and retention policies via integration tests.
acceptance_criteria: All PostgreSQL data is encrypted at rest.
Sensitive PII fields are encrypted at the application level before storage.
Data retention and deletion policies are implemented and enforced.
Automated cleanup jobs remove expired data as per policy.
GDPR/data protection compliance review is completed and documented.
Tests confirm encryption and data deletion are effective.

title: Harden File Uploads: Image Sanitization, Malware Scanning, and Metadata Stripping
description: Secure the social media photo upload pipeline by stripping EXIF metadata, re-encoding images, validating image headers, scanning uploads for malware, and implementing rate limiting on the upload endpoint.
subtasks: Integrate EXIF metadata stripping (e.g., using sharp or exiftool) for all uploaded images.
Re-encode uploaded images to a clean format before storage.
Validate that image headers match the declared content type (not just file extension).
Integrate ClamAV or equivalent malware scanning for all uploads.
Implement rate limiting on the photo upload endpoint.
Test uploads with images containing EXIF metadata, polyglot files, and known malware samples.
Run OWASP ZAP scan against the upload endpoint.
acceptance_criteria: All uploaded images have EXIF metadata stripped before storage.
Images are re-encoded and validated for correct content type.
Malware scanning is performed on all uploads and blocks malicious files.
Rate limiting is enforced on the upload endpoint.
OWASP ZAP scan shows no file upload vulnerabilities.
Test uploads confirm all controls are effective.

title: Implement Scoped Authorization, Rate Limiting, and Audit Logging for Morgan AI Agent
description: Restrict Morgan AI agent's backend access by implementing per-tool authorization, delegating end-user identity, enforcing rate limits, allow-listing tools per skill context, detecting prompt injection, and logging all tool invocations with user context.
subtasks: Implement per-tool authorization checks that validate the end-user's permissions before tool invocation.
Delegate caller identity from Morgan to backend services instead of using a super-admin account.
Add rate limiting on MCP tool invocations per session/user.
Define and enforce allow-lists of tools per skill context.
Integrate prompt injection detection/mitigation in the agent's input processing pipeline.
Log all tool invocations with originating user context for audit.
Test unauthorized tool invocations, rate limit enforcement, and prompt injection scenarios.
Run security tests simulating prompt injection and privilege escalation attempts.
acceptance_criteria: Morgan agent enforces per-tool authorization and user delegation.
Rate limiting is active on all tool invocations.
Allow-lists restrict tool access by skill context.
Prompt injection attempts are detected and blocked.
All tool invocations are logged with user context.
Security tests confirm controls are effective and no privilege escalation is possible.

title: Mandate Parameterized Queries and SQL Injection Prevention Across All Services
description: Enforce the use of parameterized queries in all database access code, enable SQL query logging in development, and add integration tests for SQL injection payloads on all user-facing endpoints.
subtasks: Update implementation guidelines to require parameterized queries (SQLx in Rust, pgx in Go) for all database operations.
Enable SQL query logging in development environments to detect string concatenation in queries.
Review all existing code for raw SQL usage and refactor to parameterized queries where needed.
Add integration tests that send SQL injection payloads to all user-facing endpoints.
Document SQL injection prevention practices in the developer guide.
Run automated security tests for SQL injection on all endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: All database queries use parameterized statements.
SQL query logging is enabled in development and reviewed for unsafe patterns.
Integration tests confirm endpoints are not vulnerable to SQL injection.
Security documentation covers SQL injection prevention requirements.

title: Adopt External Secret Store and Harden Kubernetes Secrets Management
description: Integrate an external secret store (e.g., AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault) via external-secrets.io, enable etcd encryption at rest, and ensure RBAC restricts Secret access. Migrate all API keys, database credentials, and service tokens to the external store.
subtasks: Resolve the secret management decision point and select an external secret store.
Deploy and configure external-secrets.io in the cluster.
Migrate all existing Kubernetes Secrets to be managed via ExternalSecret resources.
Enable etcd encryption at rest in the Kubernetes cluster.
Review and update RBAC policies to restrict Secret read access to only necessary ServiceAccounts.
Test secret rotation and access controls.
Document the new secret management architecture and procedures.
acceptance_criteria: All sensitive secrets are managed via an external secret store and synced with external-secrets.io.
etcd encryption at rest is enabled and verified.
RBAC policies restrict Secret access to the minimum required.
Secret rotation and access controls are tested and effective.
Documentation is updated to reflect the new secret management process.

title: Enforce Container Image Scanning, Provenance, and Admission Controls
description: Integrate container image scanning (e.g., Trivy, Grype, Snyk) into CI/CD, pin all base images to digests, verify third-party image sources, and enforce image policies via a Kubernetes admission controller (Kyverno or OPA Gatekeeper).
subtasks: Integrate automated container image scanning into the CI/CD pipeline for all services.
Pin all Docker base images to specific digests, not tags.
Scan all third-party images (e.g., Signal-CLI) and rebuild from source if provenance is unclear.
Deploy and configure a Kubernetes admission controller to enforce image signature and registry policies.
Document image scanning and admission control policies.
Test admission controller by attempting to deploy an unsigned or unapproved image.
acceptance_criteria: All container images are scanned for vulnerabilities before deployment.
Base images are pinned to digests.
Third-party images are verified and scanned.
Admission controller blocks non-compliant images.
Documentation covers image security policies.
Test deployments of non-compliant images are blocked as expected.

title: Implement Dependency Pinning and Automated Vulnerability Auditing
description: Mandate exact dependency version pinning in lock files, run dependency audit tools (cargo audit, npm audit, govulncheck) in CI, and establish a recurring job for vulnerability scanning and patch management.
subtasks: Update all project initialization tasks to require lock files (Cargo.lock, go.sum, bun.lockb) committed to the repo.
Integrate dependency audit tools (cargo audit, npm audit, govulncheck) into CI pipelines for all services.
Establish a recurring CI job to scan for new vulnerabilities and notify maintainers.
Document the process for updating and patching dependencies.
Test CI pipelines by introducing a known vulnerable dependency and verifying detection.
acceptance_criteria: All projects have lock files committed and exact dependency versions pinned.
CI runs dependency audits on every build and on a schedule.
Vulnerability findings are tracked and addressed promptly.
Documentation covers dependency management and patching procedures.
Test vulnerabilities are detected and reported in CI.

title: Harden Signal-CLI Credentials and Persistent Data Protection
description: Encrypt the Signal-CLI data directory on the PersistentVolumeClaim, restrict pod access via NetworkPolicies, monitor for anomalous Signal activity, and document procedures for revoking and re-registering the Signal number.
subtasks: Enable encryption for the Signal-CLI data directory using an encrypted StorageClass or application-level encryption.
Apply NetworkPolicies to restrict access to the Signal-CLI pod to only the Morgan agent.
Implement monitoring for unexpected Signal message patterns (e.g., high volume, unknown recipients).
Document and test the procedure for revoking and re-registering the Signal number in case of compromise.
Test pod access controls and encryption by simulating unauthorized access attempts.
acceptance_criteria: Signal-CLI data directory is encrypted at rest.
Only the Morgan agent can access the Signal-CLI pod.
Monitoring detects and alerts on anomalous Signal activity.
Revocation and re-registration procedures are documented and tested.
Unauthorized access attempts are blocked and logged.

title: Enforce Secure Google Calendar API Credential Handling and Least-Privilege Access
description: Prefer OAuth2 user consent flow for Google Calendar API integration, or if service account is used, scope it to minimum required calendars. Store credentials in tightly-scoped Kubernetes Secrets and regularly audit API access logs.
subtasks: Update RMS service to use OAuth2 user consent flow for Google Calendar API, or restrict service account access to specific calendars.
Store Google API credentials in a dedicated, namespace-scoped Kubernetes Secret accessible only to the RMS service.
Implement regular auditing of Google Calendar API access logs.
Test calendar integration with both OAuth2 and service account flows.
Document credential management and access review procedures.
acceptance_criteria: Google Calendar API credentials are stored securely and scoped to minimum required access.
OAuth2 user consent flow is used where possible.
API access logs are regularly audited.
Documentation covers credential management and access review.
Tests confirm only authorized access to calendar data.

title: Enforce Kubernetes Secret and Persistent Data Encryption for Third-Party Integrations
description: Ensure all third-party integration credentials (e.g., Signal-CLI, Stripe, Google) are stored in encrypted Kubernetes Secrets and persistent volumes. Apply RBAC and NetworkPolicies to restrict access.
subtasks: Audit all third-party integration credentials and ensure they are stored in encrypted Kubernetes Secrets.
Enable encryption at rest for all persistent volumes storing sensitive integration data.
Apply RBAC policies to restrict Secret access to only necessary ServiceAccounts.
Apply NetworkPolicies to restrict pod access to sensitive integration endpoints.
Test access controls and encryption by simulating unauthorized access attempts.
acceptance_criteria: All third-party integration credentials are encrypted at rest.
RBAC and NetworkPolicies restrict access to only authorized pods and users.
Unauthorized access attempts are blocked and logged.
Tests confirm effectiveness of encryption and access controls.

title: Develop and Document Security Incident Response Plan
description: Create a comprehensive security incident response plan and runbook covering incident classification, response procedures, communication templates, evidence preservation, and post-incident review.
subtasks: Define incident severity levels and classification criteria.
Document response procedures for each incident type (credential compromise, data breach, DDoS, unauthorized access).
Create communication templates and escalation paths for incident notification.
Define evidence preservation procedures (log retention, snapshot creation).
Establish a post-incident review process and documentation requirements.
Store the incident response plan in the security documentation repository.
Test the plan with a tabletop exercise or simulated incident.
acceptance_criteria: Incident response plan covers all required elements and is accessible to the team.
Communication templates and escalation paths are defined.
Evidence preservation and post-incident review procedures are documented.
A simulated incident exercise is conducted and lessons learned are captured.
